extra.reason: Schema in literature/db/schema.sql; CLI in scripts/lit.py with init/status/seed working and remaining subcommands stubbed pointing at their owning beads tasks. DB at literature/db/papers.sqlite is gitignored (regeneratable from references.bib + arXiv/S2).
Moved literature/tex/ -> literature/md/rwolfthesis/tex/. Stray Garjani-Ardonne jpegs were already in their subfolder, just untracked; will be ingested via H4.

Manifest in literature/db/seed_existing_pdfs.json; ingest in scripts/lit_ingest_existing.py. 40 PDFs -> 36 canonical (4 duplicates removed: Bonkhoff arxiv version, Sacramento smaller, al.-20-One-dimensional bit-identical to PhysRev87, al.-20-untitled smaller Poilblanc 2011). All renames recorded in file_provenance with sha256.
36 PDFs ingested at status=have_pdf with tier guesses (T1=10, T2=7, T3=8, T4=6, T5=8). 8 anchor seeds marked (5 with PDFs, 3 metadata-only stubs for Hollands/Stottmeister/Shi-Senthil).

lit.py extended with export bib/survey/gaps. literature/SURVEY.md (39 papers) and references.bib (39 entries) generated. Old catalog.md, overview.md, review.md moved to literature/_archive/ with explanatory README.

lit add (kind=arxiv|doi) implemented in scripts/lit.py. S2 primary, OpenAlex fallback, arXiv API for arxiv-id metadata enrichment (title is authoritative for arxiv papers, S2 sometimes contaminated with LaTeX). Idempotent: matches existing rows by arxiv/doi/s2_paper_id. Authors normalised into authors+paper_authors. Tested on Hollands, Stottmeister, Shi-Senthil, Garjani-Ardonne, Huston-et-al — all enriched/inserted correctly. S2 unauthenticated rate limit (HTTP 429) is heavy; arXiv enrichment is the reliable workhorse for arxiv-id papers.

lit fetch-cites <id> implemented via OpenAlex (DOI-first resolution then arXiv-search then title-search). Backward via referenced_works (bulk-fetched in chunks of 50). Forward via cites filter, paginated cursor-style. Idempotent: stub papers created for new dst/src nodes; edges deduped by (src, dst, source). Crawl events logged. Cross-checked against 8 anchor seeds: 502 citation edges harvested, 411 stub papers created (need C9 triage + T1 enrichment). Caveat: OpenAlex returns 0 backward refs for some recent arXiv preprints (e.g. Stottmeister 2201.11562); will supplement via S2 or PDF parsing once T3/T4 land. S2 not yet wired due to rate-limiting; OpenAlex carrying the load.

lit pdf <id> arxiv-direct path implemented (no auth, no rate limit). Tested on 4 stub seeds (Hollands, Stottmeister anyon-RG, Shi-Senthil, Huston-Penneys) — all downloaded successfully. Added --all-arxiv flag for bulk-pulling every arxiv stub. scripts/lit_fetch_pdf.mjs is the headed-Playwright fallback for paywalled (TIB VPN), pattern adapted from FQHE/scripts/fetch_aps_papers.mjs; not extensively tested but the path is wired (uses persistent profile, citation_pdf_url meta-tag heuristic, manual Cloudflare). Needs 'npm install playwright' at project root if invoked.

lit md <id> wraps marker_single (from /home/tobiasosborne/Projects/archivum/.venv/bin). Output structure: literature/md/{slug}/{slug}.md plus a {slug}_meta.json (image extraction disabled for compact output, ~30-50KB per paper). --all flag bulk-converts every paper with PDF and no md_path, ordered by tier+year. Smoke-tested on Stottmeister 2201.11562: 64s for 5-page PDF, clean markdown with formulas, figures, references.

Garjani-Ardonne 2016 (1608.04927) chased via lit fetch-cites: 57 backward refs (53 new + 1 in DB), 2 forward citing works. Caveats: only 4 GS citations confirmed historically; OpenAlex shows 2. Pair-creation direction remains essentially unexplored.

Poilblanc 2011 (1112.5950) and 2012 (1210.5605) chased: 41+83 backward refs, 12+14 forward citing. Catalog claimed 18 GS for 1210.5605; OpenAlex shows 14 — close. Includes Kirchner 2025, Soni-Troyer-Poilblanc 2015, Ayeni-Pfeifer-Brennen 2017, Finch-Frahm-Lewerenz-Milsted-Osborne 2014.
Hollands 2022 (2205.15243) chased: 68 backward refs (61 new), 2 forward citing. Categorical / subfactor formalisation. Forward-cite count is low because recent (2022).

Stottmeister 2022 anyon-RG (2201.11562) chased: forward only — 1 citing work. Caveat: OpenAlex returns 0 backward refs for this preprint (known issue). Backward refs supplemented by adding 1901.06124, 2002.01442, 2107.13834 (OAR foundation) as separate seeds.

Shi-Zhang-Senthil 2025 (2505.02893) chased: 134 backward refs (97 new), 2 forward citing. The 7 follow-up burst papers added (Pichler-Vishwanath 2506.08000, Han-Vishwanath 2508.14894, Schleith-Khalaf 2506.11211, Shi-Senthil 2506.02128 + 2512.17996, Kuhlenkamp 2509.02675, Zhang 2506.00110, Seo-Cho 2602.03848). Pichler-Vishwanath also forward-chased: 65 backward, 9 forward. Other burst papers 0/0 (very recent preprints). Divic et al 2410.18175 also linked.
Nakajima-Mehta-Goldman 2025 (2508.14961) chased: 0 forward citing (very recent, Aug 2025). Nakajima provides complementary dilute approach using ONLY quantum dimensions + fusion rules (no F-symbols).

Pre-2013 lineage automatically harvested via OpenAlex cite chase: Moore-Read 1991 (Nonabelions in FQH), Read-Rezayi 1999 (Beyond paired QH), Wilczek 1982 (Quantum mech. fractional spin), Witten 1989 (QFT and Jones), Frohlich-Marchetti 1990 (Braid stats in local QFT), Fredenhagen-Rehren-Schroer 1989 (Superselection sectors), Halperin 1984 (Fractional statistics + QHE), Bonderson-Kitaev-Shtengel 2008 (Interferometry of non-abelian anyons), Trebst-Troyer-Wang Fibonacci intro, Etingof-Nikshych-Ostrik 2005, Leinaas-Myrheim 1977. Tiered as T1/T3 accordingly. Bonderson PhD thesis (Caltech 2007) and Wen book remain TODO (manual ingest, no arxiv).

Adjacent traditions covered. Newly added: Keilmann 2011 abelian anyon-Hubbard (1009.2036), Bonkhoff 2024 1D anyon-Hubbard (2410.00089), Pfeifer-Corboz 2010 MERA anyons (1006.3532), Belletete et al 2020 RSOS defects (2003.11293), Finch-Flohr-Frahm 2017 so(n)_2 chain (1710.09620). Already in DB from cite-chase: Buican-Gromov 1701.02800, Chandran ETH 1607.00388, Trebst 0801.4602 collective states, Ardonne 1012.1080 Yang-Lee, Singh-Pfeifer-Vidal-Brennen 1311.0967 MPS for anyons, Ayeni-Singh-Pfeifer-Brennen 1509.00903 sim-braiding-MPS. All tiered.

High-impact stubs from cite chase manually tiered: 23 T1 (incl. Moore-Read 1991, Read-Rezayi 1999, Read-Green 2000, Halperin 1984, plus the 9 burst papers and Eck thesis); 11 T2 (dense-chain ancestry); 19 T3 (categorical foundations: Huston-Penneys, Trebst-Troyer-Wang, Fredenhagen-Rehren-Schroer, Bonderson interferometry, Witten 1989, Wilczek 1982, Etingof, plus OAR triple); 8 T4 (numerics); 19 T5 (Majorana experiments + abelian anyon-Hubbard + Tonks-Girardeau). Untiered=550 are mostly cite-chase stubs that don't need full triage (they're contextual references that arrived from backward-cite scans). Triage classification informed by paper titles, abstracts (where present), and OpenAlex venue fields.

Per-paper standardised entries written for the 10 anchor papers (deep notes in papers.notes column, surface in SURVEY.md). Plus a dedicated literature/SYNTHESIS.md narrative review structured as: (1) two living lineages (categorical/dense-chain vs itinerant-anyon/FQH-quasihole); (2) pre-2013 forerunners; (3) adjacent traditions; (4) gap diagram; (5) what our project uniquely provides; (6) strategic positioning.

Gap diagram in literature/SYNTHESIS.md section 4 (table across {arbitrary FC, variable N, multiplicities, OBC, braid-in-H, isometric fine-grain, continuum limit} for the 9 closest-prior-art papers + our project). Cells with X mark gaps closed by our work.
